[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a maker has signed a Merkle root R containing N offers, supply a crafted ratifierData where the leafHash of a non-existent offer equals an internal node hash of the tree (second-preimage via keccak256(left||right) without domain separation on internal nodes), triggering Midnight.take with an offer never authorized by the maker, violating the invariant that ratifier approval must bind the correct maker/market/offer, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation because the taker borrows against a market with LLTV/oracle terms not consented to by the maker? Proof idea: fuzz (left, right) pairs such that keccak256(left||right) == hashOffer(crafted_offer) and assert isLeaf returns true for the crafted offer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: cancelRoot] Can an unprivileged authorized operator O (isAuthorized[maker][O] == true in Midnight), under the precondition that maker M has live signed roots with active sell offers, call cancelRoot(M, root) to permanently cancel M's roots without M's knowledge, then take a sell offer from M using a different ratifier (e.g. SetterRatifier) that O also controls, violating the invariant that only the maker should be able to revoke their own offer authorization, causing scoped impact: maker's sell offers are DoS'd while attacker fills them through an alternate ratifier path? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: isRatified] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the EcrecoverRatifier is deployed on a chain that shares chainid with another chain (e.g. L2 testnet/mainnet collision), replay a signature produced on chain A on chain B because the domain separator keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(this))) is identical on both chains, violating the invariant that signatures must not be cross-chain replayable, causing scoped impact: unauthorized take on chain B creating bad debt in a market the maker never intended to participate in?
